# MCP Server | API Docs | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/guides/mcp-server/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Mapbox MCP Server
=================

Current version:`v0.1.0`

*   check
    
    Get driving and walking directions
    
*   check
    
    Generate isochrones and static map images
    
*   check
    
    Look up addresses and points of interest
    
*   check
    
    Reverse geocode coordinates
    

[Install](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/guides/mcp-server/#installation)
[githubContribute on GitHub](https://github.com/mapbox/mcp-server)

The **Mapbox MCP Server** exposes [Mapbox web services APIs](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/guides/)
 in the [**Model Context Protocol**](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/introduction)
, an open standard for AI tools to connect with external services. You can run the MCP Server locally or deploy it to a cloud service, and it can be used with any AI model that supports the Model Context Protocol.

Installation[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/guides/mcp-server/#installation "Direct link to Installation")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Requirements:

*   You will need a [Mapbox Access Token](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/access-token/)
     to use the MCP Server.
*   Node.js version 18 or later is required to build and run the MCP Server.

Use Mapbox MCP Server[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/guides/mcp-server/#use-mapbox-mcp-server "Direct link to Use Mapbox MCP Server")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Mapbox MCP Server can be used in several ways:

*   Option 1: Quick Start with npm
*   Option 2: Local Deployment

### Quick Start with `npm`[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/guides/mcp-server/#quick-start-with-npm "Direct link to quick-start-with-npm")

You must configure consuming applications to connect to the Mapbox MCP Server. This typically involves referencing the MCP Server’s NPM package via npx and setting your Mapbox Access Token as an environment variable.

For example, Claude Desktop users can configure MCP servers by adding the `mcpServers` key to their `claude_desktop_config.json` file:

    {  "mcpServers": {    "MapboxServer": {      "command": "npx",      "args": [ "-y", "@mapbox/mcp-server"],      "env": {        "MAPBOX_ACCESS_TOKEN": "YOUR_MAPBOX_ACCESS_TOKEN"      }    }  }}

Note: If you use NVM (Node Version Manager) to manage multiple versions of Node.js, Claude may not automatically resolve the correct version when launching the MCP Server via npx.

To make sure the server runs using the same Node.js version as npx, you should explicitly add the correct Node.js `PATH` to the `env` section of your `claude_desktop_config.json` configuration.

For example, if your system uses Node.js v18.16.0 under NVM, add the following:

    {  "mcpServers": {    "MapboxServer": {      "command": "npx",      "args": [ "-y", "@mapbox/mcp-server"],      "env": {        "MAPBOX_ACCESS_TOKEN": "YOUR_MAPBOX_ACCESS_TOKEN",        "PATH": "/Users/your-username/.nvm/versions/node/v18.16.0/bin:${PATH}"      }    }  }}

Consult the documentation of your AI model or application for specific instructions on how to configure MCP servers.

Installation guides are available for the following applications in the public repository:

*   [Claude Desktop](https://github.com/mapbox/mcp-server/blob/main/docs/claude-desktop-setup.md)
    
*   [VS Code with Copilot](https://github.com/mapbox/mcp-server/blob/main/docs/vscode-setup.md)
    
*   [Smolagents](https://github.com/mapbox/mcp-server/tree/main/docs/using-mcp-with-smolagents)
    

### Local Deployment[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/guides/mcp-server/#local-deployment "Direct link to Local Deployment")

To use Mapbox MCP Server with local Node.js involves two main steps:

1.  Clone the MCP Server code repository and build the project.
2.  Configure a consuming application to run and connect to the MCP Server, providing your Mapbox Access Token.

A Mapbox Access Token is required

You will need a **Mapbox Access Token** to use the MCP Server. An access token associates your use of Mapbox Services with your account. If you don't have one, you can [create a free account](https://www.mapbox.com/signup/)
, then copy your access token from the [Access Tokens page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/access-tokens/)
.

#### Clone and build the MCP Server[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/guides/mcp-server/#clone-and-build-the-mcp-server "Direct link to Clone and build the MCP Server")

1.  Clone the repository:

    $git clone https://github.com/mapbox/mcp-server.git

2.  Change into the project directory:

    $cd mcp-server

3.  Install dependencies:

    $npm install

4.  Build the project:

    $npm run build

The built MCP Server is a node.js script that will be located at `dist/index.js` in the project directory. You will need to reference this location when configuring consuming applications.

Docker Configuration

You may also build and run the MCP Server using Docker. See the [`Dockerfile`](https://github.com/mapbox/mcp-server/blob/main/Dockerfile)
 in the project repository.

#### Configure the consuming application[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/guides/mcp-server/#configure-the-consuming-application "Direct link to Configure the consuming application")

You must configure consuming applications to connect to the Mapbox MCP Server. This typically involves specifying the path to the MCP Server node.js file and providing your Mapbox Access Token as an environment variable.

For example, Claude Desktop users can configure MCP servers by adding the `mcpServers` key to their `claude_desktop_config.json` file:

    {  "mcpServers": {    "MapboxServer": {      "command": "node",      "args": ["/Users/username/github-projects/mcp-server/dist/index.js"],      "env": {        "MAPBOX_ACCESS_TOKEN": "YOUR_MAPBOX_ACCESS_TOKEN"      }    }  }}

Consult the documentation of your AI model or application for specific instructions on how to configure MCP servers.

Installation guides are available for the following applications in the public repository:

*   [Claude Desktop](https://github.com/mapbox/mcp-server/blob/main/docs/claude-desktop-setup.md)
    
*   [VS Code with Copilot](https://github.com/mapbox/mcp-server/blob/main/docs/vscode-setup.md)
    
*   [Smolagents](https://github.com/mapbox/mcp-server/tree/main/docs/using-mcp-with-smolagents)
    

Tools[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/guides/mcp-server/#tools "Direct link to Tools")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Mapbox MCP Server provides the following tools, which provide various geographic services such as searching for places, calculating routes, and generating maps. Each tool is powered by a specific [Mapbox Web Services API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/)
, and you can use them to enhance your AI-powered applications with geographic capabilities.

| Tool | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `CategorySearchTool` | Searches for geographic categories such as countries, regions, or postal codes. Useful for narrowing down search queries to a specific type of place. Powered by the [Search Box API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/search/search-box/) |
| `DirectionsTool` | Calculates optimal routes between waypoints for driving, walking, or cycling. Powered by the [Directions API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/navigation/directions/)<br>. |
| `ForwardGeocodeTool` | Converts a place name or address into geographic coordinates. Powered by the [Geocoding API – Forward Geocoding](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/search/geocoding/#forward-geocoding-with-search-text-input)<br>. |
| `IsochroneTool` | Calculates areas reachable within a specified travel time from a location. Powered by the [Isochrone API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/navigation/isochrone/)<br>. |
| `MatrixTool` | Computes travel times and distances between multiple points. Ideal for optimizing logistics. Powered by the [Matrix API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/navigation/matrix/)<br>. |
| `POISearchTool` | Finds points of interest such as restaurants, gas stations, or landmarks near a given location. Powered by the [Search Box API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/search/search-box/)<br>. |
| `ReverseGeocodeTool` | Converts geographic coordinates into a readable address or place name. Powered by the [Geocoding API – Reverse Geocoding](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/search/geocoding/#reverse-geocoding)<br>. |
| `StaticMapTool` | Provides static map images of a specified location and zoom level with marker, circle, line, and polygon data overlays. Powered by the [Static Images API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/static-images/)<br>. |

Was this page helpful?YesNo

# symbol layer | Help | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/symbol-layer/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

A **symbol layer** is a [layer](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/layer/)
 in which [icons](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/icon/)
 or text are added to a Mapbox map. To mark the location of a geographical point in a symbol layer, you can add icons, text, or both icons and text.

You can have all your symbols on a single symbol layer, or you can separate them out by type (for example, you could have your train station icons on one layer and your restaurant icons on a different layer). How you organize your symbols is up to you and is dependent on how you want to style your icons or text labels. Icons or text that are styled the same way can typically share a symbol layer.

**Related resources:**

*   [Mapbox Style Specification symbol layer documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/layers/#symbol)
    
*   [Mapbox Maps SDK for iOS symbol layer documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/guides/styles/work-with-layers/#symbol-layer)
    
*   [Mapbox Maps SDK for Android symbol layer documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/maps/guides/styles/work-with-layers/#symbol-layer)
    

_Examples of how to style symbol layers:_

*   [Add an icon to a map](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/example/add-image/)
    
*   [Display and style rich text labels](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/example/display-and-style-rich-text-labels/)
    
*   [Change the case of labels](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/example/change-case-of-labels/)
    
*   [Filter symbols by text input](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/example/filter-markers-by-input/)
    
*   [Center map on a clicked symbol](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/example/center-on-symbol/)

# monthly active users | Help | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/monthly-active-users/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

The Mapbox Maps and Navigation SDKs for iOS, Android, or Unity are billed by **monthly active users (MAU)**.

MAUs accumulate throughout an account's [billing period](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/invoices/#billing-period)
 and can be reviewed on the account's [statistics page](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/statistics/)
. Since we do not collect cross-device identifiers to protect end user privacy, we count each application or device as a separate MAU.

The type of MAUs displayed on an account's statistics page and associated [invoices](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/invoices/)
 are determined by which versions of the Mapbox SDKs are installed in the applications. For more information about **Maps SDK MAUs** and **Navigation SDK MAUs** see the pricing guides in the relevant product documentation:

| MAU metric | Pricing Guide |
| --- | --- |
| Maps SDK MAUs | [Maps SDK for iOS](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/guides/pricing/)<br>, [Maps SDK for Android](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/maps/guides/pricing/) |
| Navigation SDK MAUs | [Navigation SDK for iOS](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/navigation/guides/pricing/)<br>, [Navigation SDK for Android](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/pricing/) |

For more information about **Legacy SDK for Mobile MAUs**, see the [pricing by product](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/pricing/#mobile-applications-using-older-sdk-versions)
 documentation.

**Related resources:**

*   [Pricing page](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing/#session-user-pricing)
    
*   [Statistics and usage](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/statistics/)
    

Was this page helpful?YesNo

# bounding box | Help | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/bounding-box/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

A **bounding box** is a way to display a rectangular area of a map. It is expressed as a set of coordinate pairs, with the first coordinate pair referring to the southwestern corner of the box (the minimum [longitude and latitude](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/lat-lon/)
) and the second referring to the northeastern corner of the box (the maximum longitude and latitude).

Below is a visualization of a bounding box created around the state of Vermont from our [Location Helper](https://labs.mapbox.com/location-helper)
 tool.

Bounding boxes are useful for describing the spatial limits of geographic features. For example, a you could create a bounding box to describe the limits of an administrative boundary polygon, or describe the limits of a set of point features.

Once a bounding box is defined, you can use it to set the initial view of a map to make sure that the area within the bounding box is fully visible regardless of aspect ratio. You can also use a bounding box to control the view of an existing map programmatically.

### Mapbox GL JS[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/bounding-box/#mapbox-gl-js "Direct link to Mapbox GL JS")

This [Mapbox GL JS](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/mapbox-gl-js/)
 snippet creates a bounding box using the [`LngLatBounds` method](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#lnglatbounds)
 and then calls [`fitBounds`](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/map/#map#fitbounds)
 to move the map's camera, ensuring that the bounding box is fully in view.

    const southWest = new mapboxgl.LngLat(-73.9876, 40.7661);const northEast = new mapboxgl.LngLat(-73.9397, 40.8002);const boundingBox = new mapboxgl.LngLatBounds(southWest, northEast);map.fitBounds(boundingBox);

[EXAMPLE\
\
Fit map to a bounding box\
\
This GL JS example shows you how to use `fitBounds` to zoom & pan to a bounding box.](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/example/fitbounds/)
[EXAMPLE\
\
Initialize a map with a bounding box\
\
This GL JS example demonstrates how a bounding box can be used to center a map regardless of the map's aspect ratio.](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/example/initialize-with-bounding-box/)

### Mobile SDKs[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/bounding-box/#mobile-sdks "Direct link to Mobile SDKs")

Our mobile SDK's for [iOS](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/guides/)
, [Android](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/maps/guides/)
 & [Flutter](https://docs.mapbox.com/flutter/maps/guides/)
 use the `CoordinateBounds` class to create a bounding box and use the `camera` and `setCamera` methods of the `mapboxMap` object to set the camera view to the bounding box.

iOS

Android

Flutter

iOS

Android

Flutter

    let boundingBox = CoordinateBounds(southwest: CLLocationCoordinate2D(latitude: -0.90204, longitude: -158.63249),northeast: CLLocationCoordinate2D(latitude: 66.77350, longitude: 10.68889))// Center the camera on the boundslet camera = mapView.mapboxMap.camera(for: boundingBox, padding: .zero, bearing: 0, pitch: 0)

iOS

Android

Flutter

    let boundingBox = CoordinateBounds(Point.fromLngLat(-158.63249 -0.90204),Point.fromLngLat(10.68889, 66.77350),false)// Define camera boundsprivate val cameraBoundsOptions = CameraBoundsOptions.Builder().bounds(boundingBox).minZoom(10.0).build()// Fit camera to the bounding boxmapboxMap.setCamera(cameraBoundsOptions)

iOS

Android

Flutter

    let boundingBox = CoordinateBounds(southwest: Point( coordinates: Position(42.73834, -73.45338)),northeast: Point( coordinates: Position(45.02491,  -71.48691)),infiniteBounds: false)// Center the camera on the bounding boxlet camera = mapboxMap.cameraForCoordinateBounds(boundingBox);mapboxMap.setCamera(camera);

Note

A bounding box's edges are not explicitly parallel on all map [projections](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/guides/projections/)
 as they follow longitude & latitude. In this image you can see that the bounding box edges curve to follow longitude/latitude in the [`globe`](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/guides/projections/#globe)
 projection.

**Related resources:**

Any Mapbox library that creates maps or initiates geocoding requests has a class or object for using bounding boxes in your code:

*   [Mapbox Geocoding API `bbox` parameter documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/search/geocoding/#forward-geocoding-with-search-text-input)
    
*   [Mapbox GL JS `LngLatBounds` documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#lnglatbounds)
    
*   [Mapbox Maps SDK for iOS `CoordinateBounds` documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/11.13.0/documentation/mapboxmaps/coordinatebounds/)
    
*   [Mapbox Maps SDK for Android `CoordinateBounds` class documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/maps/api/11.13.0/mapbox-maps-android/com.mapbox.maps/-coordinate-bounds/)
    
*   [Mapbox Java SDK `MapboxGeocoding.Builder.bbox()` method documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/java/api/libjava-services/7.0.0/com/mapbox/api/geocoding/v5/MapboxGeocoding.Builder.html#bbox-java.lang.String-)
    
*   [Mapbox Search SDK for iOS `BoundingBox` documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/search/api/core/2.9.0-rc.2/Structs/BoundingBox.html)
    
*   [Turf.js `turf.bbox` documentation](https://turfjs.org/docs/api/bbox)
    
*   [Location helper - interact with a map and get bounding box values to use in your code.](https://labs.mapbox.com/location-helper)
    

Was this page helpful?YesNo

# MapView  Reference

[MapView\
=======](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.1/MapView.html#/MapView)

*   `[MapView](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.1/MapView.html#/c:@M@MapboxMaps@objc(cs)MapView) `
    
    Undocumented
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.1/Classes/MapView.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[MapInitOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.1/MapView.html#/c:@M@MapboxMaps@objc(cs)MapInitOptions) `
    
    Options used when initializing `[MapView](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.1/Classes/MapView.html) `.
    
    Contains the `[ResourceOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.1/Structs/ResourceOptions.html) `, `MapOptions` (including `GlyphsRasterizationOptions`) that are required to initialize a `[MapView](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.1/Classes/MapView.html) `.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.1/Classes/MapInitOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[MapInitOptionsProvider](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.1/MapView.html#/c:@M@MapboxMaps@objc(pl)MapInitOptionsProvider) `
    
    A protocol used to provide `[MapInitOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.1/Classes/MapInitOptions.html) ` when initializing a `[MapView](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.1/Classes/MapView.html) ` with a Storyboard or a nib.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.1/Protocols/MapInitOptionsProvider.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    

[### ResourceOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.1/MapView.html#/ResourceOptions)

*   `[ResourceOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.1/MapView.html#/s:10MapboxMaps15ResourceOptionsV) `
    
    Options to configure access to a resource
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.1/Structs/ResourceOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[ResourceOptionsManager](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.1/MapView.html#/s:10MapboxMaps22ResourceOptionsManagerC) `
    
    Convenience class that manages a global `[ResourceOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.1/Structs/ResourceOptions.html) `
    
    It’s possible to create `ResourceOptionsManager` instances as you need them, however it’s convenient to use the default object (`default`).
    
    For example, we recommend that the Mapbox access token be set in `application(_:didFinishLaunchingWithOptions:)` rather than relying on the value in your application’s Info.plist:
    
        ```
        func application(_ application: UIApplication, didFinishLaunchingWithOptions launchOptions: [UIApplication.LaunchOptionsKey: Any]?) -> Bool {
            // Override point for customization after application launch.
            ResourceOptionsManager.default.resourceOptions.accessToken = "overridden-access-token"
            return true
        }
        ```
        
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.1/Classes/ResourceOptionsManager.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[MapOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.1/MapView.html#/c:objc(cs)MBMMapOptions) `
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.1/Extensions/MapOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[GlyphsRasterizationOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.1/MapView.html#/c:objc(cs)MBMGlyphsRasterizationOptions) `
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.1/Extensions/GlyphsRasterizationOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[InterfaceOrientationProvider](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.1/MapView.html#/s:10MapboxMaps28InterfaceOrientationProviderP) `
    
    A protocol that supplies current interface orientation for the specified view.
    
    Use this protocol when the map view is used in non-application target (e.g. application extension target).
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.1/Protocols/InterfaceOrientationProvider.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[AttributionURLOpener](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.1/MapView.html#/s:10MapboxMaps20AttributionURLOpenerP) `
    
    A protocol to open attribution URLs.
    
    Use this protocol when the map view is used in non-application target(e.g. application extension target).
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.1/Protocols/AttributionURLOpener.html)
    
    #### Declaration

# Navigation SDK v2 | iOS | Mapbox

[メインコンテンツまでスキップ](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/navigation/v2/guides/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Navigation SDK for iOS
======================

現在のバージョン：`v2.20.0`[変更履歴を表示](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-navigation-ios/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md)

*   check
    
    Navigation UI components ready to integrate into your application
    
*   check
    
    Professionally designed map styles
    
*   check
    
    Worldwide driving, cycling, and walking directions
    
*   check
    
    Traffic avoidance and proactive rerouting
    

[githubGithubで貢献](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-navigation-ios)

A newer version of the Navigation SDK is available

This page uses 2.20.0 of the Mapbox Navigation SDK. A newer version of the SDK is available. Learn about the latest version, 3.10.1, in the [Navigation SDK documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/navigation/guides/)
.

The Navigation SDK for iOS allows you to build a complete navigation experience with the power of the [Mapbox Directions API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/navigation/directions/)
. You can build a complete turn-by-turn navigation UI inside your application using our pre-built components or use the core logic directly to build something truly custom.

The Navigation SDK provides a collection of features that are critical when building navigation projects, including:

*   Navigation routes
*   Accurate device location updates
*   Voice instruction announcements
*   Real-time user progress to their destination
*   Off-route detection and rerouting
*   Dynamic camera adjustments during turn-by-turn navigation
*   Pre-built UI components to show changing navigation information
*   Support for navigating with limited or no network connection

Types of navigation[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/navigation/v2/guides/#types-of-navigation "Types of navigation への直接リンク")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Navigation SDK offers two types of navigation: turn-by-turn navigation and free-drive navigation.

### Turn-by-turn navigation[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/navigation/v2/guides/#turn-by-turn-navigation "Turn-by-turn navigation への直接リンク")

Turn-by-turn navigation, also referred to as _active guidance_, is likely what comes to mind when you think about a typical navigation application: a user receives turn-by-turn instructions as they progress along the route. Read more in the [Turn-by-turn navigation](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/navigation/v2/guides/turn-by-turn-navigation/)
 guides.

### Free-drive navigation[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/navigation/v2/guides/#free-drive-navigation "Free-drive navigation への直接リンク")

Free-drive navigation, also referred to as _passive navigation_, is a unique Mapbox Navigation SDK feature that allows drivers to take advantage of some navigation features without selecting a route and following turn-by-turn instructions to a set destination. Read more in the [Free-drive navigation](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/navigation/v2/guides/free-drive/)
 guide.

Requirements[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/navigation/v2/guides/#requirements "Requirements への直接リンク")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Mapbox Navigation SDK is compatible with applications that are:

*   Built in Xcode 13.3.1 or higher
*   Written in Swift 5.6 or higher
*   Run on iOS 12 or higher

Use with other Mapbox tools[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/navigation/v2/guides/#use-with-other-mapbox-tools "Use with other Mapbox tools への直接リンク")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Mapbox Navigation SDK for iOS is designed to be used with several other Mapbox products.

### Directions API[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/navigation/v2/guides/#directions-api "Directions API への直接リンク")

The Navigation SDK uses the Mapbox Directions API to generate routes between two or more coordinates. The Mapbox Directions for Swift framework has many route options like setting a profile identifier (for example driving, driving with traffic, or walking), waypoint heading (to influence the direction in which a route will begin), and `roadClassesToAvoid` (for example, avoiding tolls). Read more about Mapbox [Directions for Swift in the API documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/directions/api/2.14.1/)
.

### Maps SDK for iOS[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/navigation/v2/guides/#maps-sdk-for-ios "Maps SDK for iOS への直接リンク")

By default, the Navigation SDK comes with access to the Mapbox Maps SDK for iOS. The Navigation SDK uses the Maps SDK to display routes on a map and show the user's location on the map throughout their trip to provide both navigation-related context and nearby places that may be of interest to the driver. The Navigation SDK uses Mapbox Maps SDK v10, you can find documentation for your version in the [Maps SDK API references listing](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/api-reference/)
.

Conditions[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/navigation/v2/guides/#conditions "Conditions への直接リンク")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Attribution[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/navigation/v2/guides/#attribution "Attribution への直接リンク")

If you use a Mapbox map in your application, you are required to provide attribution according to the [Mapbox Maps SDK documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/guides/#attribution)
.

### Terms[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/navigation/v2/guides/#terms "Terms への直接リンク")

The Mapbox Navigation SDK is distributed under [Mapbox's Terms of Service](https://www.mapbox.com/legal/tos)
.

### Pricing[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/navigation/v2/guides/#pricing "Pricing への直接リンク")

Read more about pricing for the Navigation SDK in the [pricing guide](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/navigation/v2/guides/pricing/)
.

Older versions of the SDK[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/navigation/v2/guides/#older-versions-of-the-sdk "Older versions of the SDK への直接リンク")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Looking for an older version of the Navigation SDK? See the [Navigation SDK v1 documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/legacy/navigation/guides/)
.

# Geodcoding APIプレイグラウンド | Developer Playgrounds | Mapbox

[メインコンテンツまでスキップ](https://docs.mapbox.com/playground/ja/geocoding/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Geocoding v6

これはMapbox Geocoding の最新バージョンであるバージョン6のドキュメントです。 以前のバージョンをお探しの場合は、[Geocoding v5 APIプレイグラウンド](https://docs.mapbox.com/playground/ja/geocoding-v5/)
を参照してください。

[**Mapbox Geocoding API**](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/search/geocoding-v6/)
を使用すると、名前（フォワード検索）または座標（リバース検索）で住所や場所を検索できます。

[フォワード](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/search/geocoding-v6/#forward-geocoding)
ジオコーディングAPIリクエストを生成するには、「forward」`search type`を選択し、「30 Rockefeller Plaza」のような住所や「Orlando、Florida」のような場所を検索します。各検索要素を個別に定義する場合は、「structured」`search type`を選択します。

[リバース](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/search/geocoding-v6/#reverse-geocoding)
ジオコーディングAPIリクエストを生成するには、「reverse」`search type`を選択し、`longitude`および`latitude`の値を入力します。地図上の任意の場所をクリックすることで、リバースジオコードリクエストも生成できます。

より良い結果を得るために、`proximity`、`language`、および`country`などのオプションパラメータで検索設定を調整します。`types`パラメータフィルタを使用して、希望の場所タイプに制限されたフィーチャの返却を制限します。

Related resources:

*   [Geocoding APIドキュメント](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/search/geocoding-v6/)
    
*   [このマップを改善する](https://www.mapbox.com/contribute/)

# MapboxCoreMaps  Reference

MapboxCoreMaps
==============

An Objective-C library that powers customizable vector maps in native applications by taking stylesheets that conform to the [Mapbox Style Specification](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/style-spec/)
, applying them to vector tiles that conform to the [Mapbox Vector Tile Specification](https://github.com/mapbox/vector-tile-spec/)
, and rendering them using Metal API.

---

MapboxCoreMaps
==============

An Objective-C library that powers customizable vector maps in native applications by taking stylesheets that conform to the [Mapbox Style Specification](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/style-spec/)
, applying them to vector tiles that conform to the [Mapbox Vector Tile Specification](https://github.com/mapbox/vector-tile-spec/)
, and rendering them using Metal API.

# Navigation SDK v1 | iOS | Mapbox

[メインコンテンツまでスキップ](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/legacy/navigation/guides/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Navigation SDK for iOS v1
=========================

現在のバージョン：`v1.4.2`[変更履歴を表示](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-navigation-ios/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md)

*   check
    
    A navigation UI ready to drop into your application
    
*   check
    
    Professionally designed map styles
    
*   check
    
    Worldwide driving, cycling, and walking directions
    
*   check
    
    Traffic avoidance and proactive rerouting
    

[githubGithubで貢献](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-navigation-ios)

A newer version of the Navigation SDK is available

This page uses v1.4.2 of the Mapbox Navigation SDK. A newer version of the SDK is available. Learn about the latest version, v2.20.0, in the [Navigation SDK documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/navigation/guides/)
.

The Mapbox Navigation SDK gives you all the tools you need to add turn-by-turn navigation to your application. It takes only a few minutes to drop a full-fledged turn-by-turn navigation view controller into your application. Or use the Core Navigation framework directly to build something truly custom.

Structure of the SDK[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/legacy/navigation/guides/#structure-of-the-sdk "Structure of the SDK への直接リンク")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Navigation SDK is divided into two frameworks: the **Mapbox Core Navigation framework** (`MapboxCoreNavigation`) and the **Mapbox Navigation framework** (`MapboxNavigation`).

### Mapbox Core Navigation[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/legacy/navigation/guides/#mapbox-core-navigation "Mapbox Core Navigation への直接リンク")

The **Mapbox Core Navigation framework** (`MapboxCoreNavigation`) contains the logic that allows you to receive user location updates, determine their relation to the route line, and get notifications that show the current state of the application in the form of a [`RouteProgress`](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/navigation/api/1.4.2/Classes/RouteProgress.html)
 object.

You can use `MapboxCoreNavigation` directly in your project to build a completely custom navigation UI or use it via the **Mapbox Navigation framework** (`MapboxNavigation`), which is powered by `MapboxCoreNavigation`.

### Mapbox Navigation[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/legacy/navigation/guides/#mapbox-navigation "Mapbox Navigation への直接リンク")

The **Mapbox Navigation framework** (`MapboxNavigation`) is the ready-made turn-by-turn navigation user interface (UI). Use `MapboxNavigation` to get up and running quickly with an in-app navigation experience. With a few lines of code, add a drop-in UI, automatic day-to-night map switching, and automatic rerouting in traffic. Adjust the style of various elements of the ready-made UI, react to location-related events as they occur, and adjust various aspects of the map view’s appearance.

Requirements[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/legacy/navigation/guides/#requirements "Requirements への直接リンク")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Mapbox Navigation SDK and Core Navigation are compatible with applications written in Swift 5 in Xcode 10.2 and above. The Mapbox Navigation and Mapbox Core Navigation frameworks run on iOS 10.0 and above.

Available resources[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/legacy/navigation/guides/#available-resources "Available resources への直接リンク")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

For further details, consult the guides and examples included with this API reference. Documentation for the Mapbox Navigation SDK for iOS comes in the form of:

*   [API documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/navigation/api/1.4.2/)
    
*   [Examples](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/legacy/navigation/examples/)
    
*   [Tutorials and troubleshooting guides](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/)
    

We welcome your [bug reports, feature requests, and contributions](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-navigation-ios/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)
. If you can't find what you're looking for, [reach out](https://www.mapbox.com/contact/support/)
 to our support team.

---

# Static Images API プレイグラウンド | Developer Playgrounds | Mapbox

[メインコンテンツまでスキップ](https://docs.mapbox.com/playground/ja/static/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

[**Mapbox Static Images API**](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/static-images/)
は特定の場所にズームインした地図の画像を生成できます。

たとえば、不動産アプリケーションはこのAPIによって生成された静的画像を使用してユーザーに不動産リストの周辺エリアを表示することができます。インタラクティブが必要ない場合、静的マップは読み込みが高速で、クライアントやネットワークリソースを少なく消費するため理想的です。

これらの画像はPNGまたはJPEG形式で表示され、インタラクティブな地図を読み込むよりもWebやモバイルアプリケーション上での効率が良くなります。

以下のプレイグラウンドでは、地図の中心、ズームレベル、画像サイズなどを実験することができます。生成されるAPI呼び出しは画像URLであり、Webやモバイルアプリケーションでホストされた画像と同じように使用できます。

Related resources:

*   [Static Images API ドキュメント](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/#static-images)
    
*   [静的マップの仕組み](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/how-mapbox-works/static-maps)
    
*   [この地図を改善してください](https://www.mapbox.com/contribute/)

Tilesets

# data-driven styling | Help | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/data-driven-styling/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Data-driven styling** allows you to style spatial data based on its properties. For example, with data-driven styling you can change the radius of a circle at an intersection based on the number of pedestrians crossing the intersection, change the color of a state polygon based on the population of each state, or use conditional logic to create bilingual labels. Data-driven styling is available in the following products:

*   [Mapbox Studio](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/mapbox-studio/)
    
*   [Mapbox GL JS](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/mapbox-gl-js/)
    
*   [Mapbox Maps SDK for iOS](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/mapbox-ios-sdk/)
    
*   [Mapbox Maps SDK for Android](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/mapbox-android-sdk/)
    

**Related resources:**

*   Getting started: [Data-driven styles](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/dive-deeper/map-design/#data-driven-styles)
    

_Mapbox Maps SDK for iOS_

*   [Expressions documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/guides/styles/use-expressions/)
    

_Mapbox Maps SDK for Android_

*   [Expressions documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/maps/guides/styles/use-expressions/)
    

_Mapbox GL JS_

*   Example: [Style circles with a data-driven property](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/example/data-driven-circle-colors/)
    
*   Tutorial: [Get started with Mapbox GL JS expressions](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/tutorials/mapbox-gl-js-expressions/)
    

Was this page helpful?YesNo

# runtime styling | Help | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/runtime-styling/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Runtime styling** allows you to dynamically change your map's [style](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/style/)
 properties in real time, allowing you to customize every aspect of the map’s appearance. This means that every element of the map can be added, removed, and styled at runtime. Runtime styling is available in all products that rely on [Mapbox GL](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/mapbox-gl/)
.

You can build custom visualizations by accessing your map's individual layers and manipulating them in response to user input or changing context. For example, you can lighten or darken a map based on the time of day, personalize the colors of parks based on user activity, or even change label languages based on user preference.

**Related resources:**

*   Getting started: [Layers and runtime styling in mobile apps](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/dive-deeper/mobile-apps/#layers-and-runtime-styling)
    
*   [Mapbox Maps SDK for iOS runtime styling documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/guides/styles/work-with-layers/)
    
*   [Mapbox Maps SDK for Android runtime styling documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/maps/guides/styles/work-with-layers/)
    

Was this page helpful?YesNo

# Tilequery APIプレイグラウンド | Developer Playgrounds | Mapbox

[メインコンテンツまでスキップ](https://docs.mapbox.com/playground/ja/tilequery/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

[**Mapbox Tilequery API**](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/#tilequery)
を使用すると、指定された経度と緯度に基づいてベクトルタイルセットからフィーチャを取得できます。このエンドポイントでは、クエリに半径を追加して近くのフィーチャを取得したり、出力をカスタマイズするための他のオプションパラメータを追加することもできます。

Tilequery APIリクエストを生成するには、各パラメータの値を入力するか、下のマップをクリックしてポイントでクエリを行います。

カスタムタイルセットの場合は、`mapbox.mapbox-streets-v8`または`mapbox.mapbox-terrain-v2`をクエリしたいタイルセットの[タイルセットID](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/glossary/tileset-id/)
で置き換えてください。

Note

Mapbox Tilequery APIエンドポイントの利用は、1分間に600リクエストまでのレート制限があります（より高いレート制限が必要な場合は、[お問い合わせください](https://www.mapbox.com/contact/sales/)
）。1分間のリクエスト制限を超えると、`HTTP 429 Too Many Requests`応答が返されます。レート制限ヘッダーに関する情報は、[Mapbox APIドキュメント](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/#rate-limit-headers)
のレート制限セクションを参照してください。

Related resources:

*   [Tilequery APIドキュメント](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/#tilequery)
    
*   [Tilequery APIチュートリアルを使用してタイムゾーンファインダーを作成する方法](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/tutorials/create-a-timezone-finder-with-mapbox-tilequery-api/)
    
*   [Tilequery APIチュートリアルを使用して標高を検出する方法](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/tutorials/find-elevations-with-tilequery-api/)
    
*   [Tilequery APIと Geocoding APIを使用してヘルシーフードファインダーを作成する方法](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/tutorials/tilequery-healthy-food-finder/)
    
*   [この地図を改善する](https://www.mapbox.com/contribute/)
    

Loading...

# ガイド | Navigation SDK v3 | iOS | Mapbox

[メインコンテンツまでスキップ](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/navigation/guides/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Navigation SDK v3
=================

現在のバージョン：`v3.10.1`[変更履歴を表示](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-navigation-ios/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md)

*   check
    
    Raw location signal enhancement
    
*   check
    
    Routing and rerouting, both online and offline
    
*   check
    
    Traffic, incidents, and closures avoidance
    
*   check
    
    Alternative routes generation, also continuously during the drive
    
*   check
    
    Route line rendering
    
*   check
    
    Navigation camera controls
    
*   check
    
    Voice instructions playback
    

[インストール](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/navigation/guides/install/)
[githubGithubで貢献](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-navigation-ios)

Mapbox Navigation SDK v3 が一般提供されました

Mapbox Navigation SDK v3 は現在、一般提供されています。Navigation SDK v2の以前のバージョンを利用してビルドを継続したい場合は、[専用ページ](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/navigation/v2/guides/)
でドキュメントをご覧いただけます。

Navigation SDK for iOS を使用すると、[Mapbox Directions API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/navigation/#directions)
、[Mapbox Map Matching](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/navigation/map-matching/)
、および[Mapbox Maps](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/guides/)
のパワーを活用して、カスタムナビゲーションエクスペリエンスを構築できます。

要件[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/navigation/guides/#requirements "要件 への直接リンク")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Navigation SDK v3 for iOS は、以下の条件を満たすアプリケーションと互換性があります：

*   Swift 5.9以上で構築されている。
*   Xcode 15.0以上で構築されている。

MapboxNavigationCoreフレームワークは、iOS 14.0以上で動作します。

* * *

# Troubleshooting Mapbox Maps and Navigation SDKs for iOS installation | Help | Mapbox

[メインコンテンツまでスキップ](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/ios-sdk-installation/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Learn how to troubleshoot issues when installing the Mapbox Maps or Navigation SDK for iOS with Swift Package Manager, CocoaPods, and Carthage.

General troubleshooting[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/ios-sdk-installation/#general-troubleshooting "General troubleshooting への直接リンク")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If you see `401` responses after your app builds successfully, make sure that you've [configured all credentials correctly](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/guides/install/#configure-credentials)
. Note that installing Maps SDK v6.0.0, Navigation SDK v1.0.0, or Search SDK v1.0.0 or greater via Carthage or CocoaPods requires **two access tokens**: one to download the SDK and another to authenticate map requests.

Swift Package Manager[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/ios-sdk-installation/#swift-package-manager "Swift Package Manager への直接リンク")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Swift Package Manager (SPM) is the recommended method for installing the Maps SDK. If you run into issues while installing the framework with SPM, try following these steps:

*   Xcode may fail to resolve SPM artifacts with errors such as `artifact of binary target 'MapboxCommon' failed extraction: The operation couldn’t be completed. (TSCBasic.StringError error 1.)`. If these errors occur, try resetting the SPM cache by opening the **File** menu in Xcode and selecting **Swift Packages** > **Reset Package Cache**. You may also need to delete your derived data folder.

CocoaPods[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/ios-sdk-installation/#cocoapods "CocoaPods への直接リンク")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Though Swift Package Manager is the preferred method for installing the Maps SDK, if you're already using CocoaPods to manage other dependencies in your project, you can also install the Mapbox SDKs for iOS in the same way.

If you run into issues while installing the framework with CocoaPods, try following these steps:

*   Use the most recent version of CocoaPods. Beta versions are not recommended for use with the Mapbox Maps SDK for iOS.
*   If `pod install` gives you an “Unable to find a specification” error, try running `pod repo update` first.
*   When you run `pod install`, CocoaPods generates an `xcworkspace` file that includes its configuration. The `xcworkspace` file has a white Xcode icon, unlike the `xcodeproj` file which has a blue Xcode icon. Make sure you're opening this file and not the original Xcode project.
*   If you're getting a permission error or having trouble installing CocoaPods itself, consult the [CocoaPods troubleshooting](https://guides.cocoapods.org/using/troubleshooting.html)
     documentation.

If you're still having issues using Mapbox with CocoaPods, [contact support](https://www.mapbox.com/contact)
 with the complete details of the issue, including error messages.

### Mapbox Maps SDK[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/ios-sdk-installation/#mapbox-maps-sdk "Mapbox Maps SDK への直接リンク")

*   If you see the error `Mapbox.h not found`, this can typically be resolved by building your application despite the error.
    
*   If you're using version v6.0.0 of the SDK or later, you will need to authorize your download of the Maps SDK with a secret access token with the `DOWNLOADS:READ` scope. Otherwise, you will see the following error:
    
        [!] Error installing MapboxMapscurl: (22) The requested URL returned error: 401 Unauthorized
    
    To resolve this, create a secret access token from the [Access Token page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/access-tokens/)
     in your Developer Console, and follow the instructions outlined in the [installation guide](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/guides/install/)
     to configure your secret token.
    

### Mapbox Navigation SDK[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/ios-sdk-installation/#mapbox-navigation-sdk "Mapbox Navigation SDK への直接リンク")

*   If you're using version v1.0.0 of the SDK or later, you will need to authorize your download of the Navigation SDK with a secret access token with the `DOWNLOADS:READ` scope. Otherwise, you will see the following error:
    
        [!] Error installing MapboxNavigationcurl: (22) The requested URL returned error: 401 Unauthorized
    
    To resolve this, create a secret access token from the [Access Token page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/access-tokens/)
     in your Developer Console, and follow the instructions outlined in the [installation guide](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/navigation/overview/#installation)
     to configure your secret token.
    

Carthage[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/ios-sdk-installation/#carthage "Carthage への直接リンク")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Mapbox Maps SDK[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/ios-sdk-installation/#mapbox-maps-sdk-1 "Mapbox Maps SDK への直接リンク")

#### v10.0.0 and higher[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/ios-sdk-installation/#v1000-and-higher "v10.0.0 and higher への直接リンク")

Installation via Carthage is not supported in the Maps SDK for iOS v10.0.0 and higher.

#### Less than v10.0.0[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/ios-sdk-installation/#less-than-v1000 "Less than v10.0.0 への直接リンク")

If you run into issues while installing the framework with Carthage, try following these steps:

*   Make sure you're using Carthage version 0.35.0 or above.
    
*   If you are encountering an error message like the one below for any Mapbox framework:
    
        *** Downloading binary-only framework MapboxMaps at "https://api.mapbox.com/downloads/v2/mobile-maps-ios/releases/ios/{VERSION_NUMBER}/MapboxMaps.zip"No available version for binary "https://s3.amazonaws.com/mapbox-ios/accounts/MapboxAccounts.json" satisfies the requirement: ~> 2.3.0
    
    You may need to verify that the dependency version specified in your `Cartfile` is correct, and then reset the `Carthage` cache:
    
        rm -rf Carthage &&rm -rf ~/Library/Caches/carthage &&rm -rf ~/Library/Caches/org.carthage.CarthageKit
    

*   If you're using version v6.0.0 of the SDK or later, you will need to authorize your download of the Maps SDK with a secret access token with the `DOWNLOADS:READ` scope. Otherwise, you will see the following error:
    
        A shell task (/usr/bin/env unzip -uo -qq -d .@site/src/mapbox-ios-sdk-dynamic.zip) failed with exit code 9:[.@site/src/mapbox-ios-sdk-dynamic.zip]End-of-central-directory signature not found.  Either this file is nota zipfile, or it constitutes one disk of a multi-part archive.  In thelatter case the central directory and zipfile comment will be found onthe last disk(s) of this archive.unzip:  cannot find zipfile directory in one of .@site/src/mapbox-ios-sdk-dynamic.zip or      .@site/src/mapbox-ios-sdk-dynamic.zip.zip, and cannot find .@site/src/mapbox-ios-sdk-dynamic.zip.ZIP, period.
    
    To resolve this, create a secret access token from the [Access Token page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/access-tokens/)
     in your Developer Console, and follow the instructions outlined in the [installation guide](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/guides/install/)
     to configure your secret token.
    
*   If you continue to run into the same error after including your `~/.netrc` file, try resetting your Carthage cache and try installing again. See cache resetting instructions above.
    

### Mapbox Navigation SDK[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/ios-sdk-installation/#mapbox-navigation-sdk-1 "Mapbox Navigation SDK への直接リンク")

#### v2.0.0 and higher[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/ios-sdk-installation/#v200-and-higher "v2.0.0 and higher への直接リンク")

`MapboxNavigation` no longer supports Carthage as of v2.0.0. If you are building a user interface from scratch, you can still install only the `MapboxCoreNavigation` framework using Carthage.

#### Less than v2.0.0[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/ios-sdk-installation/#less-than-v200 "Less than v2.0.0 への直接リンク")

*   If `Carthage` reports an error like this:
    
        Build Failed   Task failed with exit code 70:   /usr/bin/xcrun xcodebuild <...>   This usually indicates that project itself failed to compile. Check the xcodebuild log for more details: /var/folders/cx/6_wt73h5365_j0ty6085kg2m0000gn/T/carthage-xcodebuild.OahRgR.log
    
    Check that you included the `--platform ios` key in your `carthage update` command:
    
        % carthage update --platform ios --use-netrc
    

*   If you are using version v1.0.0 of the SDK or later, you will need to authorize your download of the Navigation SDK with a secret access token with the `DOWNLOADS:READ` scope. Otherwise, you will see the following error:
    
        A shell task (/usr/bin/env unzip -uo -qq -d.@site/src/mapbox-ios-sdk-dynamic.zip) failed with exit code 9:[.@site/src/mapbox-ios-sdk-dynamic.zip]End-of-central-directory signature not found.  Either this file is nota zipfile, or it constitutes one disk of a multi-part archive.  In thelatter case the central directory and zipfile comment will be found onthe last disk(s) of this archive.unzip:  cannot find zipfile directory in one of .@site/src/mapbox-ios-sdk-dynamic.zip or      .@site/src/mapbox-ios-sdk-dynamic.zip.zip, and cannot find .@site/src/mapbox-ios-sdk-dynamic.zip.ZIP, period.
    
    To resolve this, follow the instructions outlined in the [installation guide](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/navigation/overview/#installation)
     to configure your secret token.
    
*   If you continue to run into the same error after including your `~/.netrc` file, try resetting your Carthage cache and try installing again. See cache resetting instructions above.
    

CI troubleshooting[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/ios-sdk-installation/#ci-troubleshooting "CI troubleshooting への直接リンク")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If you use a dependency manager and automate builds on CI, you need to update your CI configuration. Below are the instructions to integrate the `.netrc` file into your CI:

*   **A secret access token with the `Downloads:Read` scope**.
    1.  From your account's [tokens page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/access-tokens/)
        , click the **Create a token** button.
    2.  From the token creation page, give your token a name and make sure the box next to the `Downloads:Read` scope is checked.
    3.  Click the **Create token** button at the bottom of the page to create your token.
    4.  The token you've created is a _secret token_, which means you will only have one opportunity to copy it somewhere secure.
*   Add an `SDK_REGISTRY_TOKEN` environment variable to your CI system of choice.
*   In your CI configuration file (or in a Script step), insert a new run step before fetching dependencies to the build job which runs the following commands:
    
        echo "machine api.mapbox.comlogin mapboxpassword ${SDK_REGISTRY_TOKEN}" >~/.netrc
    
*   If the project uses Carthage, change the carthage bootstrap invocation to pass in `--use-netrc`. Add this flag to the Additional Options field of the Carthage step.
*   Note that the secret access token in the `~/.netrc` file is sensitive and should not be exposed in any public CI logs. Refer to your continuous integration provider’s documentation on how to keep your secret access token from appearing in public logs.

このpageは役に立ちましたか？はいいいえ

# Other  Reference

[Other\
=====](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.2/Other.html#/Other)

*   `[Cancelable](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.2/Other.html#/s:10MapboxMaps10CancelableP) `
    
    A type that conforms to `Cancelable` typically represents a long running operation that can be canceled. Custom implementations must behave correctly if `[cancel()](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.2/Protocols/Cancelable.html#/s:10MapboxMaps10CancelableP6cancelyyF) ` is invoked more than once.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.2/Protocols/Cancelable.html)

# Display buildings in 3D | Mapbox GL JS | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/example/3d-buildings/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This example uses [`addLayer`](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/map/#map#addlayer)
 to add a [`fill-extrusion`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/layers/#fill-extrusion)
 layer that displays building heights in 3D.

The data source for the building heights is the [Mapbox Streets](https://docs.mapbox.com/vector-tiles/reference/mapbox-streets-v8/#layer-reference)
 vector tileset, which provides data for the `building` layer in the [`light-v11`](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/styles/#mapbox-styles)
 classic style.

Use Mapbox Standard for improved 3D experience

This example shows how to manually add a 3D buildings layer to a classic style. Consider using the [Mapbox Standard Style](https://docs.mapbox.com/map-styles/standard/)
 which includes 3D buildings by default, along with detailed 3D models for landmarks.

JavaScript

React

JavaScript

React

    <!DOCTYPE html><html><head><meta charset="utf-8"><title>Display buildings in 3D</title><meta name="viewport" content="initial-scale=1,maximum-scale=1,user-scalable=no"><link href="https://api.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/v3.13.0/mapbox-gl.css" rel="stylesheet"><script src="https://api.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/v3.13.0/mapbox-gl.js"></script><style>body { margin: 0; padding: 0; }#map { position: absolute; top: 0; bottom: 0; width: 100%; }</style></head><body><div id="map"></div><script>	// TO MAKE THE MAP APPEAR YOU MUST	// ADD YOUR ACCESS TOKEN FROM	// https://account.mapbox.com	mapboxgl.accessToken = 'YOUR_MAPBOX_ACCESS_TOKEN';    const map = new mapboxgl.Map({        // Choose from Mapbox's core styles, or make your own style with Mapbox Studio        style: 'mapbox://styles/mapbox/light-v11',        center: [-74.0066, 40.7135],        zoom: 15.5,        pitch: 45,        bearing: -17.6,        container: 'map',        antialias: true    });    map.on('style.load', () => {        // Insert the layer beneath any symbol layer.        const layers = map.getStyle().layers;        const labelLayerId = layers.find(            (layer) => layer.type === 'symbol' && layer.layout['text-field']        ).id;        // The 'building' layer in the Mapbox Streets        // vector tileset contains building height data        // from OpenStreetMap.        map.addLayer(            {                'id': 'add-3d-buildings',                'source': 'composite',                'source-layer': 'building',                'filter': ['==', 'extrude', 'true'],                'type': 'fill-extrusion',                'minzoom': 15,                'paint': {                    'fill-extrusion-color': '#aaa',                    // Use an 'interpolate' expression to                    // add a smooth transition effect to                    // the buildings as the user zooms in.                    'fill-extrusion-height': [                        'interpolate',                        ['linear'],                        ['zoom'],                        15,                        0,                        15.05,                        ['get', 'height']                    ],                    'fill-extrusion-base': [                        'interpolate',                        ['linear'],                        ['zoom'],                        15,                        0,                        15.05,                        ['get', 'min_height']                    ],                    'fill-extrusion-opacity': 0.6                }            },            labelLayerId        );    });</script></body></html>

This code snippet will not work as expected until you replace `YOUR_MAPBOX_ACCESS_TOKEN` with an access token from [your Mapbox account](https://account.mapbox.com/)
.

JavaScript

React

    import React, { useEffect, useRef } from 'react';import mapboxgl from 'mapbox-gl';import 'mapbox-gl/dist/mapbox-gl.css';const MapboxExample = () => {  const mapContainerRef = useRef();  const mapRef = useRef();  useEffect(() => {    // TO MAKE THE MAP APPEAR YOU MUST    // ADD YOUR ACCESS TOKEN FROM    // https://account.mapbox.com    mapboxgl.accessToken = 'YOUR_MAPBOX_ACCESS_TOKEN';    mapRef.current = new mapboxgl.Map({      style: 'mapbox://styles/mapbox/standard',      config: {        basemap: {          theme: 'monochrome'        }      },      center: [-74.0066, 40.7135],      zoom: 15.5,      pitch: 45,      bearing: -17.6,      container: 'map',      antialias: true    });    mapRef.current.on('style.load', () => {      const layers = mapRef.current.getStyle().layers;      const labelLayerId = layers.find(        (layer) => layer.type === 'symbol' && layer.layout['text-field']      ).id;      mapRef.current.addLayer(        {          id: 'add-3d-buildings',          source: 'composite',          'source-layer': 'building',          filter: ['==', 'extrude', 'true'],          type: 'fill-extrusion',          minzoom: 15,          paint: {            'fill-extrusion-color': '#aaa',            'fill-extrusion-height': [              'interpolate',              ['linear'],              ['zoom'],              15,              0,              15.05,              ['get', 'height']            ],            'fill-extrusion-base': [              'interpolate',              ['linear'],              ['zoom'],              15,              0,              15.05,              ['get', 'min_height']            ],            'fill-extrusion-opacity': 0.6          }        },        labelLayerId      );    });    return () => mapRef.current.remove();  }, []);  return <div id="map" ref={mapContainerRef} style={{ height: '100%' }}></div>;};export default MapboxExample;

This code snippet will not work as expected until you replace `YOUR_MAPBOX_ACCESS_TOKEN` with an access token from [your Mapbox account](https://account.mapbox.com/)
.

More Examples[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/example/3d-buildings/#more-examples "Direct link to More Examples")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

View some examples of our other style features here:

\
Rain Example Mapbox GL JS\
\
This example adds a rain effect to the map by setting the style's rain property. You can adjust the intensity, direction, color, and more of the precipitation presented on screen.](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/example/rain/)
[EXAMPLE\
\
Snow Example Mapbox GL JS\
\
This example adds a snow effect to the map by setting the style's snow property. You can adjust the intensity, direction, color, and more of the precipitation presented on screen.](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/example/snow/)

# MapView  Reference

[MapView\
=======](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.2/MapView.html#/MapView)

*   `[MapView](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.2/MapView.html#/c:@M@MapboxMaps@objc(cs)MapView) `
    
    Undocumented
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.2/Classes/MapView.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[MapInitOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.2/MapView.html#/c:@M@MapboxMaps@objc(cs)MapInitOptions) `
    
    Options used when initializing `[MapView](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.2/Classes/MapView.html) `.
    
    Contains the `[ResourceOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.2/Structs/ResourceOptions.html) `, `MapOptions` (including `GlyphsRasterizationOptions`) that are required to initialize a `[MapView](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.2/Classes/MapView.html) `.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.2/Classes/MapInitOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[MapInitOptionsProvider](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.2/MapView.html#/c:@M@MapboxMaps@objc(pl)MapInitOptionsProvider) `
    
    A protocol used to provide `[MapInitOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.2/Classes/MapInitOptions.html) ` when initializing a `[MapView](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.2/Classes/MapView.html) ` with a Storyboard or a nib.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.2/Protocols/MapInitOptionsProvider.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    

[### ResourceOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.2/MapView.html#/ResourceOptions)

*   `[ResourceOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.2/MapView.html#/s:10MapboxMaps15ResourceOptionsV) `
    
    Options to configure access to a resource
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.2/Structs/ResourceOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[ResourceOptionsManager](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.2/MapView.html#/s:10MapboxMaps22ResourceOptionsManagerC) `
    
    Convenience class that manages a global `[ResourceOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.2/Structs/ResourceOptions.html) `
    
    It’s possible to create `ResourceOptionsManager` instances as you need them, however it’s convenient to use the default object (`default`).
    
    For example, we recommend that the Mapbox access token be set in `application(_:didFinishLaunchingWithOptions:)` rather than relying on the value in your application’s Info.plist:
    
        ```
        func application(_ application: UIApplication, didFinishLaunchingWithOptions launchOptions: [UIApplication.LaunchOptionsKey: Any]?) -> Bool {
            // Override point for customization after application launch.
            ResourceOptionsManager.default.resourceOptions.accessToken = "overridden-access-token"
            return true
        }
        ```
        
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.2/Classes/ResourceOptionsManager.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[MapOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.2/MapView.html#/c:objc(cs)MBMMapOptions) `
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.2/Extensions/MapOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[GlyphsRasterizationOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.2/MapView.html#/c:objc(cs)MBMGlyphsRasterizationOptions) `
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.2/Extensions/GlyphsRasterizationOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[InterfaceOrientationProvider](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.2/MapView.html#/s:10MapboxMaps28InterfaceOrientationProviderP) `
    
    A protocol that supplies current interface orientation for the specified view.
    
    Use this protocol when the map view is used in non-application target (e.g. application extension target).
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.2/Protocols/InterfaceOrientationProvider.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[AttributionURLOpener](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.2/MapView.html#/s:10MapboxMaps20AttributionURLOpenerP) `
    
    A protocol to open attribution URLs.
    
    Use this protocol when the map view is used in non-application target(e.g. application extension target).
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.2/Protocols/AttributionURLOpener.html)
    
    #### Declaration

    It’s possible to create `ResourceOptionsManager` instances as you need them, however it’s convenient to use the default object (`default`).
    
    For example, we recommend that the Mapbox access token be set in `application(_:didFinishLaunchingWithOptions:)` rather than relying on the value in your application’s Info.plist:

        func application(_ application: UIApplication, didFinishLaunchingWithOptions launchOptions: [UIApplication.LaunchOptionsKey: Any]?) -> Bool {

# Vector Tiles API | API Docs | Mapbox

[メインコンテンツまでスキップ](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/vector-tiles/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Mapbox Vector Tiles API**は、Mapboxがホストする[ベクタータイル](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/glossary/vector-tiles/)
を提供します。

ベクタータイルの取得[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/vector-tiles/#retrieve-vector-tiles "ベクタータイルの取得 への直接リンク")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

get

https://api.mapbox.com/v4/{tileset\_id}/{zoom}/{x}/{y}.{format}

clipboard

必須パラメータ型説明`tileset_id``string``username.id`形式のベクタータイルセットの一意識別子。複数のベクタータイルセットを合成するには、最大15のタイルセットIDをカンマで区切ったリストを使用します。`zoom``integer`[Slippy Map Tilenames](http://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Slippy_map_tilenames)
仕様で説明されているように、タイルのズームレベルを指定します。`{x}/{y}``integer`[Slippy Map Tilenames](http://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Slippy_map_tilenames)
仕様で説明されているように、タイルの列`{x}`と行`{y}`を指定します。`format``string`返されるタイルの形式を指定します：

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| `.mvt` | ベクータイル |
| `.vector.pbf` | ベクタータイル |

次のオプションパラメータを使用して、このエンドポイントからの結果をさらに絞り込むことができます。

| オプションパラメータ | 型   | 説明  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `style` | `string` | スタイル最適化タイルリクエストに必要です。`style`パラメータは2つの部分で構成されており、スタイルIDと最近編集された`timestamp`が`<style ID>@<timestamp>`形式で含まれます。timestampパラメータは、Mapbox Studioで作成されたスタイルに含まれるスタイルJSONの`modified`プロパティから取得されます。 |

スタイル最適化ベクタータイルの取得

スタイルIDをタイルリクエストに含めることで、ベクタータイルはさらに最適化されます。スタイルパラメータが提供される場合、そのスタイルの ソース、[フィルター](https://docs.mapbox.com/ja/style-spec/reference/layers/#filter)
、[`minzoom`](https://docs.mapbox.com/ja/style-spec/reference/sources/#vector-minzoom)
、および [`maxzoom`](https://docs.mapbox.com/ja/style-spec/reference/sources/#vector-maxzoom)
プロパティが分析され、地図に表示されないデータが ベクタータイルから削除されます。Mapbox GL JSはMapboxホストのスタイルJSONでスタイル最適化されたベクタータイルをリクエストできます。 未使用のレイヤーやフィーチャーは最適化されたスタイルから削除されます。Mapbox GL JSやMapboxモバイルSDKを使用して実行時にスタイルを動的に 変更することを計画している場合、フィルターやズーム範囲を広げても、読み込まれたスタイルで表示されないデータもデータに含まれません。

### リクエスト例: ベクタータイルの取得[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/vector-tiles/#example-request-retrieve-vector-tiles "リクエスト例: ベクタータイルの取得 への直接リンク")

    $curl "https://api.mapbox.com/v4/mapbox.mapbox-streets-v8/1/0/0.mvt?access_token=YOUR_MAPBOX_ACCESS_TOKEN"# スタイルクエリーパラメータを使用してスタイル最適化タイルを返す$curl "https://api.mapbox.com/v4/mapbox.mapbox-streets-v8/12/1171/1566.mvt?style=mapbox://styles/mapbox/streets-v12@00&access_token=YOUR_MAPBOX_ACCESS_TOKEN"

### レスポンス: ベクタータイルの取得[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/vector-tiles/#response-retrieve-vector-tiles "レスポンス: ベクタータイルの取得 への直接リンク")

レスポンスは指定された形式のベクタータイルです。パフォーマンス向上のため、画像タイルは`max-age`ヘッダー値が12時間先に設定されています。

ベクタータイルAPIエラー[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/vector-tiles/#vector-tiles-api-errors "ベクタータイルAPIエラー への直接リンク")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| レスポンスボディ `message` | HTTPステータスコード | 説明  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `Not Authorized - No Token` | `401` | クエリでトークンが使用されていません。 |
| `Not Authorized - Invalid Token` | `401` | クエリで使用したアクセストークンを確認してください。 |
| `Forbidden` | `403` | アカウントに問題がある可能性があります。詳細については[アカウントページ](https://console.mapbox.com/)<br>を確認してください。  <br>  <br>場合によっては、URL制限を持つアクセストークンを使用することも`403`エラーを引き起こすことがあります。詳細については、[トークン管理ガイド](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/tokens/#url-restrictions)<br>をご覧ください。 |
| `Tileset {tileset name} does not exist` | `404` | クエリで使用したタイルセットの名前を確認してください。 |
| `Tile not found` | `404` | クエリでリクエストしたタイルの列と行を確認してください。 |
| `Zoom level must be between 0-30.` | `422` | クエリで指定されたズームレベルが30より大きいか、数字以外の文字が含まれています。 |
| `Tileset does not reference vector data` | `422` | クエリで指定されたタイルセットはラスタタイルセットであり、ベクタータイルセットではありません。ラスタタイルを取得するには、[ラスタタイルAPI](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/raster-tiles/)<br>を使用してください。 |

ベクタータイルAPIの制限と制約[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/vector-tiles/#vector-tiles-api-restrictions-and-limits "ベクタータイルAPIの制限と制約 への直接リンク")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   MapboxベクタータイルAPIエンドポイントのデフォルトのレート制限は1分あたり100,000リクエストです。もし、レート制限をの数を高くしたい場合は、[お問い合わせください。](https://www.mapbox.com/contact/sales/)
    .
*   レート制限を超えると、`HTTP 429 Too Many Requests`レスポンスを受け取ります。レート制限ヘッダーの情報については、[レート制限ヘッダー](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/guides/#rate-limit-headers)
    セクションをご覧ください。
*   ベクタータイルAPIからのレスポンスには、デフォルトのCache-Controlヘッダーが設定された`max-age=43200,s-maxage=300`、つまりデバイスキャッシュのTTLが12時間、CDNキャッシュのTTLが5分の設定が含まれています。新しいタイルセットデータは、バックエンドでさらに5分間キャッシュされます。
*   複数のタイルセットIDを合成する場合、APIリクエストは最も高い`s-maxage`値を持つタイルセットのキャッシュ時間を持ちます。
*   キャッシングに関する一般的な情報については、[Maps APIsキャッシングトラブルシューティングガイド](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/api-caching/)
    をご覧ください。

ベクタータイルAPIの料金[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/vector-tiles/#vector-tiles-api-pricing "ベクタータイルAPIの料金 への直接リンク")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **リクエスト** ごとに請求されます
*   料金ページの **[Maps](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing/#vectortile)
    ** セクションで、タイルリクエスト ごとの料金と割引をご確認ください。

ベクタータイルAPIの使用は**タイルリクエスト**で測定されます。無料枠に含まれるタイルリクエスト数および無料枠を超えたリクエストごとの料金の詳細は、[料金ページ](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing/#vectortile)
をご覧ください。

このpageは役に立ちましたか？はいいいえ

---

# Raster Tiles API | API Docs | Mapbox

[メインコンテンツまでスキップ](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/raster-tiles/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

The **Mapbox Raster Tiles API** serves [raster tiles](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/glossary/raster/)
 generated from satellite imagery tilesets and tilesets generated from raster data uploaded to Mapbox.com.

Retrieve raster tiles[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/raster-tiles/#retrieve-raster-tiles "Retrieve raster tiles への直接リンク")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

get

https://api.mapbox.com/v4/{tileset\_id}/{zoom}/{x}/{y}{@2x}.{format}

clipboard

Required parametersTypeDescription`tileset_id``string`Unique identifier for the raster tileset in the format `username.id`. To composite multiple tilesets, use a comma-separated list of up to 15 tileset IDs.`zoom``integer`Specifies the tile's zoom level, as described in the [Slippy Map Tilenames](http://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Slippy_map_tilenames)
 specification.`{x}/{y}``integer`Specifies the tile's column `{x}` and row `{y}`, as described in the [Slippy Map Tilenames](http://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Slippy_map_tilenames)
 specification.`format``string`Specifies the format of the returned tiles:

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| `.grid.json` | UTFGrid |
| `.png` | True color PNG |
| `.png32` | 32 color indexed PNG |
| `.png64` | 64 color indexed PNG |
| `.png128` | 128 color indexed PNG |
| `.png256` | 256 color indexed PNG |
| `.jpg` | 80% quality JPG |
| `.jpg70` | 70% quality JPG |
| `.jpg80` | 80% quality JPG |
| `.jpg90` | 90% quality JPG |
| `.webp` | 80% quality WebP |

The `format` of any image request can be replaced by any of these formats to adjust image quality for different bandwidth requirements. Higher-compression formats like `jpg70` or `png32` can be useful to favor performance over image quality.  
  
**Note:** Tiles that include `mapbox.satellite` are always delivered as JPEGs, even if the URL specifies PNG. The PNG format can't efficiently encode photographic images like those used by `mapbox.satellite`.  
  
**Note**: Some tilesets may display a black background in older browser versions but not in newer browser versions. This is because many modern browsers support the [WebP](https://developers.google.com/speed/webp/)
 image format. For more information see [Troubleshoot raster image with black background](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/raster-transparency-issues/)
.

| Optional parameters | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `@2x` | `string` | Request a higher DPI version of the image. |

### Example request: Retrieve raster tiles[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/raster-tiles/#example-request-retrieve-raster-tiles "Example request: Retrieve raster tiles への直接リンク")

    # Retrieve a 2x tile; this 512x512 tile is appropriate for high-density displays$curl "https://api.mapbox.com/v4/mapbox.satellite/1/0/0@2x.jpg90?access_token=YOUR_MAPBOX_ACCESS_TOKEN"

### Response: Retrieve raster tiles[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/raster-tiles/#response-retrieve-raster-tiles "Response: Retrieve raster tiles への直接リンク")

The response is a raster image tile in the specified format. For performance, image tiles are delivered with a `max-age` header value set 12 hours in the future.

Raster Tiles API errors[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/raster-tiles/#raster-tiles-api-errors "Raster Tiles API errors への直接リンク")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Response body `message` | HTTP status code | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `Not Authorized - No Token` | `401` | No token was used in the query. |
| `Not Authorized - Invalid Token` | `401` | Check the access token you used in the query. |
| `Forbidden` | `403` | There may be an issue with your account. Check your [Account page](https://console.mapbox.com/)<br> for more details.  <br>  <br>In some cases, using an access tokens with URL restrictions can also result in a `403` error. For more information, see our [Token management guide](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/tokens/#url-restrictions)<br>. |
| `Tileset {tileset name} does not exist` | `404` | Check the name of the tileset you used in the query. |
| `Tile not found` | `404` | Check the column and row of the tile requested in the query. |
| `Tileset does not contain any tiles` | `404` | The tileset does not yet contain any tiles. It may not have been published or is still being processed. |
| `Zoom level must be between 0-30.` | `422` | The zoom level specified in the query is larger than 30 or contains non-numeric characters. |
| `Invalid quality value {value} for raster format {jpg/png}` | `422` | The `format` specified in the query must be one of the formats listed in the [Retrieve raster tiles](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/raster-tiles/#retrieve-raster-tiles)<br> section of this documentation. |

Raster Tiles API restrictions and limits[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/raster-tiles/#raster-tiles-api-restrictions-and-limits "Raster Tiles API restrictions and limits への直接リンク")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   The default rate limit for the Mapbox Raster Tiles API endpoint is 100,000 requests per minute. もし、レート制限をの数を高くしたい場合は、[お問い合わせください。](https://www.mapbox.com/contact/sales/)
    .
*   If you exceed the rate limit, you will receive an `HTTP 429 Too Many Requests` response. For information on rate limit headers, see the [Rate limit headers](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/guides/#rate-limit-headers)
     section.
*   Responses from the Raster Tiles API set default Cache-Control headers to `max-age=43200,s-maxage=300`, or a device cache TTL of 12 hours and a CDN cache TTL of 5 minutes. New tileset data is cached for up to a further 5 minutes on the backend as well.
*   If you composite multiple tileset IDs, the API request will have the cache time of the tileset with the highest `s-maxage` value.
*   For general information on caching, see the [Maps APIs caching troubleshooting guide](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/api-caching/)
    .

Raster Tiles API pricing[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/raster-tiles/#raster-tiles-api-pricing "Raster Tiles API pricing への直接リンク")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **requests** ごとに請求されます
*   料金ページの **[Maps](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing/#tile)
    ** セクションで、tile requests ごとの料金と割引をご確認ください。

Usage of the Raster Tiles API is measured in **tile requests**. Details about the number of tile requests included in the free tier and the cost per request beyond what is included in the free tier are available on the [pricing page](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing/#tile)
.

このpageは役に立ちましたか？はいいいえ
